rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring all property definitions and constants are properly declared in the AADL model or its dependencies before use in AGREE contracts.
Resolve type mismatches in binary expressions by ensuring both operands are of the same type (int, real, or bool) and that all identifiers are properly resolved.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, only reference identifiers that are visible in the current scope and properly declared in the AADL model.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for constant and equation declarations.
Place assume/guarantee clauses in the correct AADL component scope: type contracts in component types, implementation contracts in implementations.
Avoid introducing new package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes unless they are explicitly imported via 'with' clauses.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must resolve to valid types; use 'eq' for helper declarations and 'assign' only for output features.
Binary comparisons in AGREE require operands of matching types (e.g., int >= int, real >= real).
Property references in AGREE must resolve to existing definitions in the AADL model or its configured dependencies.
AGREE contracts should not introduce new identifiers that are not visible in the current AADL component scope.
anti_patterns: Do not add new property definitions or constants to AGREE annexes unless they are already declared in the AADL model.
Do not use unresolved identifiers or invalid types in AGREE expressions; always ensure type consistency and resolution.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
